questions.question: A normal human being can hear frequencies ranging from 20 Hz to _________ Hz.
questions.explanation: The healthy human ear can hear frequencies ranging from 20Hz to 20,000 Hz.

questions.question: Jet aircrafts fly in which layer of atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Commercial jet planes fly in the lower stratosphere to avoid turbulence.

questions.question: Sphygmomanometer is used for measuring ________
questions.explanation: It is an instrument for measuring blood pressure. It is attached to an inflatable air-bladder cuff and used with a stethoscope, for measuring blood pressure.

questions.question: Light year is approximately equal to _________ Km
questions.explanation: It is a unit of astronomical distance equivalent to the distance that light travels in one year, which is 9.4607 X 10¹² km.

questions.question: Who invented induction motor?
questions.explanation: The first induction motor was invented by the Nikola Tesla in 1887.

questions.question: Control rods in nuclear reactor absorb which among the following?
questions.explanation: Control Rods have the ability to capture the slow neutrons and can control the chain reaction at any stage.

questions.question: Optical fiber works on the principle of _________
questions.explanation: Fiber optic cables can transmit light very quickly over large distances because of the principle of total internal reflection.

questions.question: Ozone in which layer of atmosphere prevents ultraviolet rays from reaching earth's surface?
questions.explanation: Ozone and oxygen molecules in the stratosphere absorb ultraviolet light from the Sun, providing a shield that prevents this radiation from passing to the Earth's surface.

questions.question: Bernoulli's principle is used in working of _________
questions.explanation: Airplanes fly as a result of Bernoulli's principle, which says that if air speeds up the pressure is lowered. Thus, a wing generates lift because the air goes faster over the top creating a region of low pressure, and thus lift.

questions.question: Which among the following fundamental forces is weakest force?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the weakest of the four fundamental forces. Strongest is the strong nuclear force.

questions.question: Which among the following reaction in sun results in release of energy and heat?
questions.explanation: The process of fusion in the Sun is known as the proton-proton chain. This reaction releases a tremendous amount of heat and energy.

questions.question: India's military base outside the country is in ________
questions.explanation: Farkhor Air Base is a military air base located near the town of Farkhor in Tajikistan.

questions.question: Indian Navy Day is observed on 4ᵗʰ of _________?
questions.explanation: Navy day in India is observed on 4 December every year to celebrate the achievements and role of the naval force to the country.

questions.question: Maitree military exercise is between India and __________?
questions.explanation: Maitree is an Indo - Thailand Military Exercise. During the exercise both the Armies are to evolve common tactical procedures and drills to enhance inter-operability.

questions.question: Which is the highest rank in Indian Air Force?
questions.explanation: The highest rank attainable in the IAF is Marshal of the Indian Air Force, conferred by the President of India after exceptional service during wartime.

questions.question: Ordnance Factories Board is headquartered in _______?
questions.explanation: Ordnance Factories Board consisting of the Indian Ordnance Factories, is an industrial organisation, functioning under the Department of Defence Production of Ministry of Defence, Government of India. It is headquartered in Kolkata.

questions.question: Who acts as the chancellor of Defence Institute of Advanced Technology?
questions.explanation: Defence Institute of Advanced Technology is the premier Deemed University under the Department of Defence Research & Development, Ministry of Defence, Government of India. Defence minister acts as the chairman of the institute.

questions.question: Which is NOT an operation of Indian Army?
questions.explanation: It is a Search Operation Undertaken by Indian Navy to find the missing Boieng 777 M.H 17 Malaysian Flight.

questions.question: Which is a short-range tactical battlefield missile?
questions.explanation: Prahaar is a solid-fuel rocket surface-to-surface guided short-range tactical ballistic missile for battlefield use developed by DRDO of India.

questions.question: Line of Actual Control borders India and _______?
questions.explanation: The Line of Actual Control (LAC) is a demarcation line that separates Indian-controlled territory from Chinese-controlled territory.

questions.question: Sino-Indian occurred in __________?
questions.explanation: The Sino-Indian War, also known as the Sino-Indian Border Conflict, was a war between China and India that occurred in 1962.

questions.question: Which is a type of asexual reproduction in plants?
questions.explanation: Types of asexual reproduction: binary fission, budding, parthenogenesis, spores and fragmentation.

questions.question: Which is an example of Marsupial?
questions.explanation: Kangaroo is a marsupial. The major difference between the marsupials and the rest of the mammals is the embryonic development pattern.

questions.question: Which blood group is referred to as "Universal Blood Donor" group?
questions.explanation: O negative donors are the "Universal Donor." People with O negative blood are universal red blood cell donors. This means that their red blood cells can be transfused to any blood type.

questions.question: An average human has ____ liters of blood.
questions.explanation: The average adult has about 4.5 to 5.7 liters of blood circulating inside their body.

questions.question: Which is referred to as break-bone disease?
questions.explanation: Dengue is an acute infectious disease caused by a virus and transmitted by the bite of the Aedes mosquito, also known as break bone fever and bone-crusher disease.

questions.question: Ketogenic body is that is low on _________
questions.explanation: Ketosis is a metabolic state of body caused by a low-carbohydrates.

questions.question: Which tissue in plants transport water?
questions.explanation: The basic function of xylem is to transport water from roots to shoots and leaves.

questions.question: Zika virus can be transmitted through _______
questions.explanation: Zika virus disease is mainly spread by mosquitoes and sexual contact.

questions.question: Which is the white layer of the eye is referred to as?
questions.explanation: The white part of the eye is a tough outer layer called the sclera.
